title: coronilla valentina subsp. valentina
intro: Coronilla valentina subsp. valentina is a perennial herb belonging to the legume family, native to the Mediterranean region and known for its bright yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Coronilla valentina subsp. valentina is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and protecting against infections. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some cases to treat kidney stones and as a diuretic.
